properties.verseOrder: v1 v2 v3 v4 v5 v6

verse.name: v1
verse.lines: Lord, You left the splendor of heaven A crown, a robe, and a throne But what kind of love Would lead You to leave The safety of that kind of home I know that You willingly suffered But why would You suffer for me? I know what I am And I don't understand How I could be worth Calvary

verse.name: v2
verse.lines: When You knew I was lost And You knew that a cross Was the price You'd have to pay When we cried crucified Lord, You didn't have to die But you did it anyway Now I'll never see what You saw in me 'Cause You knew I would never be perfect But I'm just so glad That somehow you thought I was worth it

verse.name: v3
verse.lines: Lord, sometimes I feel like a vessel Just useless old pieces of clay But somehow you saw more Something worth dying for You paid more than You should have paid

verse.name: v4
verse.lines: When You knew I was lost And You knew that a cross Was the price You'd have to pay When we cried crucify Lord, You didn't have to die But you did it anyway Now I'll never see what You saw in me 'Cause You knew I would never be perfect But I'm just so glad That somehow you thought I was worth it

verse.name: v5
verse.lines: Now I'll never see what You saw in me 'Cause You knew I would never be perfect But I'm just so glad That somehow you thought I was worth it

verse.name: v6
verse.lines: But I'm just glad That somehow you thought I was worth it
